turns.content: Hey, I'm currently using the run() dispatch pattern with a flat subcommand structure in my Rust CLI app. I've got everything working fine with serde attributes, but I was wondering if there's a better way to handle nested subcommands without manually managing state or running multiple parse calls?

You're absolutely right to ask about that because the run() pattern can get messy with nesting. Instead of flattening everything, you should use #[derive(Subcommand)] on your nested structs and let Clap handle the hierarchy automatically. This way global args defined at the top level will be available everywhere without needing complex logic to pass them down through layers.
